title: Ising surface defects can get dirty
authors: António Antunes, Apratim Kaviraj, Baishali Roy
abstract: Real critical systems, such as uniaxial ferromagnets in the 3d Ising universality class, are constrained by boundaries and subject to random couplings. We consider the Wilson-Fisher fixed point in $4-ε$ dimensions subject to a random magnetic field localized on a two-dimensional surface, which becomes co-dimension 1 in the physical $ε\to1$ limit. Using the replica method for the disordered field, we find that the ordinary boundary condition is stable under disorder but also discover a non-trivial ``dirty" boundary condition which can be reached by tuning the disorder strength or the local temperature. We also investigate the logarithmic structure of the defect spectrum and how it emerges via the replica formalism.
summary: This paper explores how random magnetic fields on a surface can create new types of boundary conditions in critical systems. It specifically shows that these 'dirty' boundaries lead to the emergence of complex logarithmic structures in the theory's spectrum. This provides deeper insight into the interplay between disorder and criticality in the 3D Ising model.
main_problem: The study investigates how localized random magnetic fields on a 2D surface defect affect the boundary conditions and stability of the 3D Ising universality class.
main_result: The authors identify a new 'dirty' boundary condition fixed point and demonstrate the emergence of logarithmic conformal field theory structures in the replica limit.
method: The researchers use the replica method for disorder averaging combined with Renormalization Group (RG) analysis and one-loop perturbative expansion in 4-epsilon dimensions.
model_or_system: A massless interacting scalar field theory (Wilson-Fisher fixed point) in 4-epsilon dimensions featuring a 2D surface defect subject to a Gaussian random magnetic field and a local temperature term.
key_observables: Anomalous dimensions of bilinear defect operators, scaling dimensions of local operators, and one-point correlation functions of bulk composite operators.
important_parameters: The expansion parameter epsilon, the number of replicas N (taking the N to 0 limit), and the defect couplings h1 and h2.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the epsilon expansion and focuses on the co-dimension 1 limit for the physical 3D case.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows Feynman diagrams for same-replica contributions; Figure 2 shows diagrams for different-replica contributions; Figure 3 displays an RG flow plot with fixed points; Figure 4 illustrates diagrams for defect correlation functions.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the model of surface defects, applies the replica method to handle disorder, performs RG calculations to find fixed points and stability, analyzes the resulting operator spectrum, and discusses the emergence of logarithmic structures.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Defect Feynman diagrams for same replica. The crosses indicate counter terms.
FIG. 2: Defect Feynman diagrams for different replicas.
FIG. 3: Flow plot for the surface defect RG flow. The two ordered fixed points are denoted by a red dot, and the disordered one by a blue dot.
